package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "net/http"
    "net/smtp"
)

// Positive scenario for smtp_plaintext_auth: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `smtp.PlainAuth` used without TLS, sending credentials in plaintext.
type CaseSmtpPlaintextAuthDTO struct {
    ID       string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status   string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount   int    `json:"amount"`
    Payload  []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseSmtpPlaintextAuthModel struct {
    ID        string       `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID  string       `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status    string       `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseSmtpPlaintextAuthRepository struct {
    db    *gorm.DB
    sql   *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseSmtpPlaintextAuthModel
}

type CaseSmtpPlaintextAuthService struct {
    repo   *CaseSmtpPlaintextAuthRepository
    client *http.Client
}

type CaseSmtpPlaintextAuthAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveSmtpPlaintextAuth(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseSmtpPlaintextAuthDTO) error {
    // PlainAuth sends credentials in plaintext without TLS.
    auth := smtp.PlainAuth("", "user@example.com", "password123", "smtp.example.com")
    err := smtp.SendMail("smtp.example.com:25", auth, "from@example.com", []string{"to@example.com"}, []byte(input))
    return err
}
